EQUIPMENT
Finding the local black market could turn into an adventure. By their very nature tnese seedy sellers are not supposed t0 pe easily found Therefore , characters looking for black mar- ket will need to use their Streetwise Skill to find the right per- Wno can get them in touch with the right people_ perhaps requiring an appropriate bribe threat: Shady contacts are also Ven useful for locating black-market outlets. Whether the equipment question even available will course be Up t0 the gamemaster: then the gamemaster should establish cost based upon its equipment ratings gen- eral, commonly available items can be had for far less than retail price that drum of lubricant just fell out of the DropShip' while rare and highly restricted items can command more than twenty or thirty times the normae going rate Of course_ Eood Negotiation Skills could help t0 bring down the price_ Characters need to exercise some discretion with their new tOys_ Most Iaw enforcement officials do not treat black market dealings lightly: characters are caught with such illega Items_ they coula be slapped with stiff fines even imprsoned depend: ing on what they are apprehended with. Black marketeers prefer maintain Jow profile the characters draw attention t0 themselves and t0 where they may have gotten their Mllega goods, the people they dealt with may decide to pay them non-cordial visit. Characters should realize that their dealings with the black market might have consequences down the road.
Ment in poOr condition. Tne old   axiom that states Buyer beware: deal that" too good to be true probably is is just as relevant the thirty first century as when was written:
The Basic or the Luxury Model The equipment Iisted in this section i5 representative countless similar products that can be found throughout known space. Some are among the most basic of items However, arti sans and machinists certainly have made some of these bor ing' pieces into beautiful and legant items that deserve to be presented as gifts of honor and passed down t0 one children as a heiroon; For example an ordinary knife costs eight bills and common item More exquisite pieces, hand made and with finely etched details are sold at much higher prices Books remain status symbo Mdmy family libraries_ Small, controlled batches of wines ad liqueurs have far bet" ter taste and aroma than do mass-produced varieties. Some vehicles even have their bodies and frames sculpted to the tastes of their wners The gamemaster must set the prices for these luxury' items and the availability rating will always be one 0r two levels higher than the standard ior Its type
USING EQUIPMENT The following sections explain terms that define equipment (Equipment Data) and the effects encumbrance: Guidelines for repair of equipment are also provided
Gray Areas There are other avenues acquiring equipment, including many that are of dubious legality: For example characters may salvage equipment from battlefield; some military types have even been known to break down entire vehicles ad ship them home piece by piece. While most militaries havc strict regula tions what their people can keep_ these rules are com: mionly Overlooked especially by officers who come home with their own trophies Police and customs agents might confiscate things that turn Up on searches, keeping choice pieces for their own collections Pernaps the most common way of circumventing local Iaws to travel where the Item question i5 not restricted and easily purchased. For example the Periphery well known for Its Iax weapons regulations. Characters can then simply return home with their new toy" assuming they can make through customs, as this constitutes smuegiing: So long as characters can justify having the items in some way ("on; picked that up while was fighting the Jade Falcons on Wolcott" and are not causing other legal problems_ they will Iikely not be hassled_
EQUIPMENT DATA Weapons and other equipment will have various ratings and mechanics tor reterence game play: Statistics for equipment be tound in the equipment tables peginning p. 153 Rules regarding the use of weapons and armor in combat can be found in Combat (p. 1091, Armor Penetratlon (AP): weapon '5 potential t0 pierce armor Armor Value (AV): The relative defensive value of ditiOI against the four types of damage (see Type. below). Cost: The cost of the item in C bills Crew: The number of trained personnel needed t0 operate the equipment: more tnan one person is required the equip ment not truly portable ad should be fixed t0 static mount vehicle Damage: Tne damage inflicted Dy the weapon in D6. Patch: The cost in C-bills for materia repair point 0i armor value (see Armor Repairs, 1331. This does not include the cost of labor: Power Usage (Pwr use): The number of standard power points the item consures per use Or per the listed interval of time . Energy weapons Iist power usage in the Shots column of Weapon Table , 153 Power Capacity: The maximum number of standard power units power pack can hold with cnarge- As items usC Up power points, this number will decrease until it is recharged or the current charge reaches zero ad the item ceases to function: Range: The   short/medium /long/extreme ranges
Used
New Not everything character picks up the course cam paign will be hot off the assembly Iline. Goods bought from private citizens and objects confiscated scavenged off the battlefield probably will have seen previous use. Some outfits might openly trade in used items, providing inexpensive way tor characters purchase equipnent otherwise outside their means The gamemaster should apply TN modifiers used equip
